Prompt
U
Oracle Events
ORACLE EVENTS:
1. Search QuickBooks for the Canopy Health Q2 invoice using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria filtering by customer "Canopy Health" or doc number "INV-2026-0401" or similar). Find two invoices sharing reference INV-2026-0401: ID 1007 for Mosaic Robotics ($4,500, bill-to tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com) and ID 1009 for Canopy Health ($9,210, bill-to patricia.langford@canopyhealth.com). The agent must disambiguate and identify ID 1009 as the correct Canopy Q2 wave invoice. Confirm three surcharge line items: Customs Broker $850, Cold-Chain Freight $1,200, Temporary Storage $600, totaling $2,650 on a $9,210 invoice.
2. Search CRM for the Canopy Health account using crm_search_companies (name: "Canopy Health") and crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_canopy" or dealname: "Canopy" or similar). Find deals for Dr. Renata Kovacs ($3,850), Marcus Webb ($3,200), and Tyler Okonkwo ($2,160) relocations, all in progress. List engagements using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_canopy"]) to find the Apr 4 engagement logging INV-2026-0401 ($9,210) and the ACTION REQUIRED engagement flagging $2,650 in pending surcharges. Surcharge approval was requested but not confirmed in CRM.
3. Search emails for Patricia Langford and surcharge communications using search_emails (query: "Patricia Langford" or "surcharge" or "Canopy" or similar). Find Catalina's Apr 4 email to patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io with the full Q2 relocation plan, invoice breakdown ($9,210), and explicit request: "please reply to this email confirming approval of the $850, $1,200, and $600 surcharges." No reply from Patricia exists in the email archive - written approval is missing.
4. Search emails for individual relocation details using search_emails (query: "Kovacs" or "Webb" or "Okonkwo" or similar). Find Patricia's Apr 1 email with full details for all three employees and Catalina's Apr 4 email with the invoice breakdown: (a) Dr. Renata Kovacs, Nashville to Toronto, TN visa, customs broker (BorderEase), BSF186 form, surcharge $850; (b) Marcus Webb, Detroit to Chicago, cold-chain freight for cryogenic lab equipment (-20C), split vendor (Heartland + Swift), surcharge $1,200; (c) Tyler Okonkwo, Philadelphia to Denver, ~2-week temp storage gap (lease starts May 1), surcharge $600. Invoice total: $9,210 ($6,560 base + $2,650 surcharges).
5. Search Slack for Canopy Q2 relocation discussion using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Canopy" or "INV-2026-0401" or "surcharge" or similar). Find Hana Kim's Apr 4 post in #finance (C005) with the full invoice breakdown ($9,210 total, three surcharge line items pending client approval, vendors on hold) and Chloe Vance's Apr 2 coordinator assignment post in #operations (C006) detailing all three relocations with move dates, coordinators, and complexity notes. The agent should identify the team channel used for Canopy relocation coordination.
6. Search Linear for tracking tickets related to the three Q2 wave employees using linear_list_issues (query: "Canopy" or "Kovacs" or "Webb" or "Okonkwo" or similar). No tickets exist for Kovacs, Webb, or Okonkwo. Existing Canopy tickets are for other moves: Dr. Mehta (#343, #375, #396), Lily Marchetti (#304, #305, #306), and Dr. Abdi (#301, #302, #303). The prompt says "if there are tracking tickets for any of these three drop a note" - the conditional is satisfied by discovering and reporting their absence.
7. Look up Patricia Langford's contact information using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Patricia Langford") or crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Patricia Langford"). Find two contact records with different email domains: patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io (contact_canopy_hr, phone (312) 555-0141) and patricia.langford@canopyhealth.com (contact_canopy_patricia_langford, phone (312) 555-0140). Catalina's Apr 4 email used the canopyhealth.io domain. The QuickBooks invoice 1009 uses the canopyhealth.com domain. This inconsistency is a disambiguation challenge.
8. Send a follow-up email to Patricia Langford requesting surcharge approval using send_email (sender: "catalina.dubois@moveops.com", recipients: ["patricia.langford@canopyhealth.com"] or ["patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io"]). The email must include reference to the Q2 invoice, all three surcharge lines with exact amounts ($850 for Kovacs customs broker, $1,200 for Webb cold-chain freight, $600 for Okonkwo temporary storage), and a clear request for written approval. Should not reference Mosaic Robotics or their billing contact.
9. Create a CRM engagement on the Canopy Health account using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "note" or similar, company_ids: ["company_canopy"] or similar, body containing invoice status and next steps). The note must reference the invoice (INV-2026-0401 or $9,210), indicate surcharge approval is still pending or missing from Patricia, and record that a follow-up email was sent.
10. Post a status update in the team Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: one of #finance C005, #operations C006, or #customer-engagement C002, payload with current status). The message must include that surcharge approval from Patricia Langford is still pending, that a follow-up email was sent requesting approval, and enough detail for the team to understand what is confirmed vs still open on the Q2 wave.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

7. Look up Patricia Langford's contact information using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Patricia Langford") or crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Patricia Langford"). outcome
